query: I just got a 50,000-row patient dataset for a binary classification task (readmission yes/no). It came as a CSV from the data engineering team. Audit it before I model.
expected_behavior: Recommends a per-column report covering at minimum: nulls (count and %), type, distinct count, and range or summary statistics
Recommends a row-level integrity check for duplicate rows (and, when an ID column exists, same-content-different-ID duplicates) — not just per-column checks
Includes a cardinality or semantic-class check that would catch a high-cardinality column being misclassified as categorical (e.g. free-text notes labeled as a category)
